title: Automate Monthly Allocations with Claude Cowork
description: Turn a multi-hour monthly allocation process into a few minutes using Claude Cowork — with built-in checks to ensure debits equal credits. Based on Brock Beyer's allocation workflow.
globalInfo: Source: Brock Beyer — LinkedIn post (2026-04-18 scrape). Key workflow: use Claude Cowork to automate monthly cost allocations. Write clear instructions like onboarding a staff accountant, build in validation checks (debits = credits, corporate balance = $0), request specific output formats, and save as a reusable Cowork Skill.

modules.title: Set Up Your Allocation Task in Cowork

steps.title: Create a New Task Called 'Allocations'
steps.content: In the Cowork desktop app, create a dedicated task for your monthly allocation work. Name it clearly — you'll reuse this every month.

steps.title: Write Clear Instructions for How Your Allocations Work
steps.content: In the task description, explain: what costs need to be allocated, which departments are impacted, the allocation basis (e.g., headcount percentage), and any special rules. Be specific — treat this like onboarding a new staff accountant.

steps.title: Upload or Connect Your Source Data
steps.content: Provide the file containing the costs to be allocated (export from your GL or ERP). If your ERP supports MCP, connect it directly so Cowork can pull the data automatically.

modules.title: Validate, Format, and Save as a Skill

steps.title: Build in Validation Checks
steps.content: Tell Cowork to: round to the nearest cent, ensure total debits equal total credits, validate that the corporate balance equals $0 after the allocating entry, and flag any allocation that seems unusual. These checks catch errors before they hit your books.

steps.title: Request Specific Output Formats
steps.content: Ask Cowork to produce: (1) a clean, formatted reconciliation showing what was allocated and how, and (2) a journal entry document in the exact format your ERP needs for upload. If you use MCP, Cowork can propose the journal entry directly in your ERP.

steps.title: Review the Output and Save as a Cowork Skill
steps.content: Check the first run carefully. Once you're satisfied, save the entire workflow as a Cowork Skill so next month you just upload the new data and run the skill. What used to take 30 minutes to several hours now takes minutes.
